  # Group triples by subject
      from collections import defaultdict
      groups = defaultdict(list)
      for t in triples:
          groups[t.subject].append(t)
  
      sentences = []
      for subj, items in groups.items():
          parts = [phrase(subj, t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
          # Join parts with commas and a final conjunction
          if len(parts) == 1:
              sent = parts[0]
          else:
              sent = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
          sentences.append(sent)
  
      # Combine subject sentences
      return " Also, ".join(sentences) + "."
with:
  # Build structures keyed by mission and astronaut
      from collections import defaultdict
  
      mission_to_astronauts = defaultdict(list)      # mission -> list of astronaut names
      astronaut_info = defaultdict(dict)            # astronaut -> predicate -> object
      mission_info = defaultdict(lambda: {"operator": None,
                                           "backupPilot": None,
                                           "commander": None,
                                           "crewMembers": []})  # mission -> extra info
  
      for t in triples:
          pred = t.predicate.lower()
          if pred == "mission":
              mission_to_astronauts[t.object].append(t.subject)
          elif pred in {"operator", "backuppilot", "commander"}:
              info = mission_info[t.subject]
              if pred == "operator":
                  info["operator"] = t.object
              elif pred == "backuppilot":
                  info["backupPilot"] = t.object
              elif pred == "commander":
                  info["commander"] = t.object
          elif pred == "crewmembers":
              mission_info[t.subject]["crewMembers"].append(t.object)
          else:
              astronaut_info[t.subject][pred] = t.object
  
      sentences = []
  
      # Create sentences centered on missions (including astronauts)
